PY183Exception HandlingExceptions separate ordinary control flow from error paths so failures can travel up the call stack until code that understands the problem handles them.
PY194FunctionsFunctions package behavior under a name, introduce a call stack frame, and return values explicitly or `None` implicitly.
PY204Arguments Deep-DivePython supports positional, keyword, variadic, keyword-only, and positional-only parameters so APIs can control both flexibility and clarity.
PY214Scope & LEGB RulePython resolves names through the LEGB chain: Local, Enclosing, Global, Builtin.
PY224Lambda FunctionsA `lambda` creates a small anonymous function expression with a single expression body.
PY234Higher-Order FunctionsHigher-order functions accept functions, return functions, or both. Python uses that idea throughout the standard library.
PY244RecursionRecursion solves a problem by reducing it to smaller versions of the same problem until a base case stops the chain.
PY255Classes & ObjectsClasses define how new objects should be built, while each object stores its own state and can expose behavior through methods.
PY265The Object ModelIn Python, everything is an object: numbers, strings, functions, classes, modules, and instances all have identity, type, and behavior.
PY275InheritanceInheritance lets one class reuse and specialize behavior from another, and Python resolves multiple inheritance with the C3 method resolution order.
PY285EncapsulationPython prefers conventions and properties over hard privacy barriers, using naming conventions and descriptors to guide access.
PY295Dunder / Magic MethodsDunder methods let your classes participate in Python syntax and built-ins by implementing agreed-upon protocol hooks.
PY305Abstract Base Classes and ProtocolsAbstract base classes define nominal interfaces, while protocols describe structural expectations that any compatible object can satisfy.
PY315DataclassesDataclasses generate repetitive class machinery such as `__init__`, `__repr__`, and comparisons for classes that are mainly structured data.
PY326The Iterator ProtocolAn iterator is any object that returns itself or another iterator from `__iter__()` and produces items from `__next__()` until it raises `StopIteration`.
PY336GeneratorsGenerators produce values lazily with `yield`, suspending and resuming execution instead of building all results eagerly.
PY346Coroutines Basics via GeneratorsBefore `async`/`await`, generator methods like `send`, `throw`, and `close` showed that suspended functions could also receive data and exceptions.
PY357DecoratorsA decorator takes a callable or class, wraps or transforms it, and returns the replacement object bound to the original name.
PY367Context ManagersContext managers pair setup with guaranteed cleanup, usually through the `with` statement and the `__enter__`/`__exit__` protocol.
PY377DescriptorsDescriptors are objects that customize attribute access through `__get__`, `__set__`, and `__delete__`.
PY387MetaclassesMetaclasses customize class creation itself. In Python, classes are objects too, and `type` is their default metaclass.
PY398ModulesA module is a Python file loaded as an object, giving code a namespace and a reusable import boundary.
PY408PackagesA package groups related modules under a shared namespace, usually as a directory and often with an `__init__.py` file.
PY418Virtual Environments & Dependency ManagementVirtual environments isolate project dependencies so one project's package versions do not silently break another's.
PY429File HandlingFile I/O opens a stream to bytes on disk and lets you choose text or binary interpretation, access mode, and encoding strategy.
PY439JSON, CSV, pickleSerialization turns Python data into a portable representation and back again, but different formats solve very different problems.
PY449pathlib vs os.path`pathlib` models filesystem paths as objects with methods and operators, while `os.path` exposes mostly string-based helper functions.
PY4510Concurrency vs Parallelism vs AsynchronyConcurrency is about dealing with multiple tasks at once, parallelism is about doing work at the same instant on multiple cores, and asynchrony is about cooperative waiting without blocking a thread.
PY4610ThreadingThreads let one process make progress on multiple tasks concurrently, but in CPython the GIL means CPU-bound pure-Python threads do not execute Python bytecode in parallel.
PY4710MultiprocessingMultiprocessing uses separate processes to achieve real CPU parallelism for pure-Python code, at the cost of separate memory spaces and serialization overhead.
PY4810asyncio`asyncio` provides cooperative concurrency with an event loop, coroutines, tasks, and non-blocking I/O-friendly APIs.
PY4911Type HintsType hints describe expected shapes of values for humans and static analyzers, even though Python itself does not enforce most annotations at runtime.
PY5011Static AnalysisStatic analyzers such as mypy and pyright read your code without running it and flag type inconsistencies, unreachable paths, and other issues earlier.
PY5112unittest`unittest` is Python's built-in xUnit-style testing framework with test case classes, fixtures, and many assertion helpers.
PY5212pytestpytest is a third-party testing framework that favors plain functions, powerful fixtures, and concise assertions.
PY5312MockingMocking replaces collaborators with controllable doubles so a test can focus on one unit's behavior without touching real external systems.
PY5413ProfilingProfiling measures where time or memory actually goes so you can optimize evidence, not guesses.
PY5513Python Memory Model Deep-DiveCPython combines reference counting, cyclic garbage collection, object caching, and object-layout choices such as `__slots__` to manage memory.
PY5613Writing Faster PythonFast Python starts with algorithmic complexity, then applies measurements and data-structure choices before micro-optimizations.
PY5714Common Design Patterns in PythonPython uses classic patterns too, but the most useful versions usually lean on functions, modules, and duck typing instead of heavy class hierarchies.
PY5814Python Standard Library TourThe standard library is large enough that knowing where to look is often more valuable than memorizing every function name.
PY5914Python Anti-PatternsMany painful Python bugs come from a handful of recurring anti-patterns that feel convenient in the moment but blur the language's real semantics.
